title: Day 12: Hegseth Vows 'Most Intense Day' of Strikes; Iran Mines Strait of Hormuz
detail: The US-Israel war on Iran entered its twelfth day as Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth vowed it would be 'yet again our most intense day of strikes inside Iran — the most fighters, the most bombers, the most strikes.' Gen. Dan Caine confirmed US forces have now struck more than 5,000 targets in Iran across 30 of its 31 provinces, with 43 Iranian naval vessels destroyed or damaged, more than 60% of missile launchers neutralized, and Iranian air defenses degraded by 80%. Iran's ballistic missile launch rate has collapsed 92% (from 480/day on Feb. 28 to ~40/day), and drone launches have fallen 92% as well.

title: Iran Lays Mines in Strait of Hormuz; US Destroys 16 Mine-Laying Vessels
detail: US intelligence confirmed Iran had begun laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz. Trump issued an ultimatum on Truth Social: 'If Iran has put out any mines in the Hormuz Strait...we want them removed, IMMEDIATELY!' and warned of 'death, fire and fury' if blocking continued. Trump announced the US had destroyed 10 'inactive mine-laying boats and/or ships' within hours. Gen. Caine confirmed CENTCOM is 'hunting and striking mine-laying vessels and mine storage facilities,' with a total of 16 mine-laying vessels eliminated. The IRGC claimed no US Navy warship had 'dared' approach the Strait of Hormuz, the Persian Gulf, or Sea of Oman during the war.

title: Oil Prices Swing Wildly: Brent Plunges 17% Then Rebounds After False Tanker Claim
detail: Oil prices saw dramatic swings on March 11 as traders tried to interpret mixed war signals. Brent crude plunged 17% to fall below $80/bbl — its biggest single-day drop in years — after reports of possible ceasefire diplomacy and IEA emergency reserve release plans. Prices then rebounded to near $90 after US Energy Secretary Chris Wright briefly posted — then quickly deleted — a claim that the US Navy had escorted an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz. The IEA is considering its largest-ever emergency strategic petroleum reserve release. Ship transits through the Strait of Hormuz have decreased 95% since March 1, with only 55 transits recorded March 1–9 vs. 160 the prior week. Saudi Aramco's CEO warned of 'catastrophic consequences' for global energy markets.

title: Iran Claims US/Israel Hit ~10,000 Civilian Sites; UN Raises Alarm on Scale
detail: Iran's UN ambassador told the Security Council that US and Israeli forces have bombed nearly 10,000 civilian sites in Iran — including approximately 8,000 residential homes, commercial centers, medical and pharmaceutical facilities, and schools — killing more than 1,300 civilians. The figure is strongly disputed by Washington and Tel Aviv, which assert all strikes target military or dual-use infrastructure. Iran's internet connectivity remained at ~1% of normal levels (240 consecutive hours offline, among the longest government-imposed internet shutdowns on record globally, per NetBlocks). OHCHR called the communications blackout a grave humanitarian concern undermining civilian protection.

title: Iran Parliament Speaker Rules Out Ceasefire; IRGC Says Iran Decides When War Ends
detail: Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf said Iran is 'definitely not looking for a ceasefire,' while IRGC spokesman Ali Mohammad Naini declared 'Iran will determine when the war ends.' Ali Larijani appeared to directly threaten Trump on X: 'Iran doesn't fear your empty threats. Even those bigger than you couldn't eliminate Iran. Be careful not to get eliminated yourself.' Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi told CNN Tehran would continue attacking Gulf countries and that the war would end only through 'economic pain.' Meanwhile, Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Gharibabadi confirmed that China, Russia, and France had contacted Tehran about a possible ceasefire, but Iran insists all attacks must stop first.

title: Lebanon Death Toll Rises to 570; 750,000 Displaced as Israeli Strikes Continue
detail: Lebanon's government disaster management office reported the death toll from Israeli strikes on Hezbollah positions surpassed 570 by March 11, including 83 children, with more than 750,000 people displaced. France called for an emergency UN Security Council meeting on Lebanon, condemning Hezbollah for joining the war while calling on Israel to 'refrain from any land-based or long-term interventions in Lebanon.' France offered the Lebanese government full support.

title: Pentagon: 140 US Personnel Wounded; Jordan Reports 119 Iranian Missiles and Drones
detail: The Pentagon confirmed approximately 140 US service members have been wounded in the conflict, stating 'the vast majority of these injuries have been minor and 108 service members have already returned to duty,' with 8 currently severely injured. Jordan announced it had been targeted by 119 Iranian missiles and drones, injuring 14 people. The cumulative picture now shows Iran distributing fire across 13 countries to maximize regional economic disruption — the UAE absorbing 1,468 projectiles vs. Israel's 392, and Kuwait 562, Bahrain 281.

title: Exxon Evacuates Non-Essential Staff; IEA Eyes Record Reserve Release
detail: ExxonMobil evacuated non-essential employees from its Middle East operations as the conflict disrupted regional energy flows. The company also scaled back some activities to manage swelling oil inventories. The International Energy Agency is reportedly considering the largest emergency release of strategic petroleum reserves in its history to help stabilize global oil supplies. G7 finance ministers announced they are prepared to release energy stockpiles to stabilize markets.
